zh.lifeSummary: Margreta Haynes是Rici Haynes和Ellan Haynes的女兒，於1583年11月出生在英國德比夏爾。她於1603年1月30日與Richardus Holmes在英國英格蘭德比夏爾斯特費爾德結婚。他們至少是2個女兒的父母。她去世在英國英格蘭德比夏爾斯特費爾德，埋葬在英國德比夏爾斯特費爾德。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: Margreta Haynes是Rici Haynes和Ellan Haynes的女儿，于1583年11月出生在英国德比夏爾。她于1603年1月30日与Richardus Holmes在英国英格兰德比夏爾斯特費爾德结婚。他们至少是2个女儿的父母。她去世在英国英格兰德比夏爾斯特費爾德，埋葬在英国德比夏爾斯特費爾德。

nameMeanings.name: Haynes
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English: variant of Hayne , with genitival or post-medieval excrescent -s; see Hain . Compare Hains .
English: habitational name from Haynes (Bedfordshire), which may derive from Old English hagenas, plural form of hagen ‘enclosure’.

English: in English counties bordering Wales, perhaps from the Welsh personal name Einws (a pet form of Welsh Einion) with a prosthetic H-; see also Enos . While this is linguistically possible there is no evidence yet to show that it was the origin of the surname.

variants.name: Hains
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  French Andre, Monique.
English (southwestern): variant of   Haynes   and, in North America, (also) an altered form of this. Compare   Hance   2 and   Hince   2.

History: Jonathan (Joseph) Haynes, born in MA, was captured in New England by the French at age twelve and taken to QC, Canada, where he married Marie Pauzé in 1712. His descendants bear various altered forms of the surname Haynes, such as Hains, Hance, and Hince.

variants.name: Hayne
meanings: History: Isaac Hayne (1745–81) was an American revolutionary militia officer, executed by the British for breaking parole. He owned an ironworks and was manufacturing ammunition for the American forces when he was caught. His grandfather had emigrated from England to SC c. 1700.

variants.name: Aynes
meanings: English: variant of Haynes with loss of initial H-. This surname is very rare in Britain.

variants.name: Haines
meanings: English (southern) and Irish (Cork): variant of   Haynes  .

variants.name: Hain
meanings: English: topographic name from Middle English heghen, a weak plural of hay, from Old English (ge)hæg ‘enclosure’, denoting someone who lived at a farm, or a habitational name from any of various places called with this word. See also Haynes .

English: from the Middle English personal name Hain, Heyne. Like Deyn this may be a rhyming pet form of Reynald (see Reginald ) or other names of Norman origin beginning with Rein- such as Reyner and Rainbert. Alternatively, the name may be a variant of Hagan .

English: nickname for a wretched individual, from Middle English hain(e), heyne, hayn ‘wretch, niggard’. In some cases however, it is possible that this name derives from a spelling (not a pronunciation) of hi(g)ne ‘servant’ (see Hine ), with the inverted spelling -ey- for i.

nameMeanings.name: Margreta
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: From the Latin form of Greek Margarītēs. See Margaret .

variants.name: Margaret
meanings: An extremely common given name from the Middle Ages onwards, derived via Old French Marguerite and Latin Margarita from Greek Margarītēs, from margaron ‘pearl’, a word ultimately of Hebrew origin. The name was always understood to mean ‘pearl’ throughout the Middle Ages. The first St Margaret was martyred at Antioch in Pisidia during the persecution instigated by the Emperor Diocletian in the early 4th century. However, there is some doubt about her name, as the same saint is venerated in the Orthodox Church as Marina . There were several other saintly bearers of the name, including St Margaret of Scotland ( d. 1093 ), wife of King Malcolm Canmore and daughter of Edmund Ironside of England. It was also the name of the wife of Henry VI of England, Margaret of Anjou ( 1430–82 ), and of Margaret Tudor ( 1489–1541 ), sister of Henry VIII , who married James IV of Scotland and ruled as regent there after his death. See also Margery , Marjorie .

variants.name: Margrethe
meanings: An extremely common given name from the Middle Ages onwards, derived via Old French Marguerite and Latin Margarita from Greek Margarītēs, from margaron ‘pearl’, a word ultimately of Hebrew origin. The name was always understood to mean ‘pearl’ throughout the Middle Ages. The first St Margaret was martyred at Antioch in Pisidia during the persecution instigated by the Emperor Diocletian in the early 4th century. However, there is some doubt about her name, as the same saint is venerated in the Orthodox Church as Marina . There were several other saintly bearers of the name, including St Margaret of Scotland ( d. 1093 ), wife of King Malcolm Canmore and daughter of Edmund Ironside of England. It was also the name of the wife of Henry VI of England, Margaret of Anjou ( 1430–82 ), and of Margaret Tudor ( 1489–1541 ), sister of Henry VIII , who married James IV of Scotland and ruled as regent there after his death. See also Margery , Marjorie .

historicEvents.title: The First English Colony is Established in America
historicEvents.description: England established its first colony in North America in 1585 as a way to generate additional wealth. The colony was named Virginia, after the virgin Queen Elizabeth I.

historicEvents.title: Elizabeth I Signs Death Warrant for Mary, Queen of Scots
historicEvents.description: Elizabeth I signed the death warrant for Mary, Queen of Scots, on February 1, 1587, as she was found guilty of plotting to assasinate Elizabeth. Mary, Queen of Scots, was beheaded at Fotheringhay Castle in England on February 8, 1587.
historicEvents.urlTitle: Elizabeth I.org - Mary Queen of Scots

historicEvents.title: The Honourable East India Company
historicEvents.description: The Honourable East India Company, a British joint-stock company, was established in 1600 for trade in the Indian Ocean region. At its height, the British East India Company had a private army which was twice the size of the British Army, ruled large sections of India, and revenues in the millions.
historicEvents.urlTitle: Historic UK - The East India Company

en.lifeSummary: Margreta Haynes was born in November 1583, in Derbyshire, England as the daughter of Rici Haynes and Ellan Haynes. She married Richardus Holmes on 30 January 1603, in Chesterfield, Derbyshire, England, United Kingdom. They were the parents of at least 2 daughters. She died in Chesterfield, Derbyshire, England, United Kingdom, and was buried in Chesterfield, Derbyshire, England.

fr.lifeSummary: Margreta Haynes est née en novembre 1583, à Derbyshire, Angleterre, fille de Rici Haynes et Ellan Haynes. Elle a épousé Richardus Holmes le 30 janvier 1603, à Chesterfield, Derbyshire, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni. Ils ont eu au moins 2 filles. Elle est décédée à Chesterfield, Derbyshire, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni, et a été enterrée à Chesterfield, Derbyshire, Angleterre.

de.lifeSummary: Margreta Haynes wurde im November 1583 in Derbyshire, England, als Tochter von Rici Haynes und Ellan Haynes geboren. Sie hat am 30. Januar 1603 in Chesterfield, Derbyshire, England, United Kingdom Richardus Holmes geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 2 Töchter. Sie starb in Chesterfield, Derbyshire, England, United Kingdom und wurde in Chesterfield, Derbyshire, England begraben.

it.lifeSummary: Margreta Haynes è nata il nel novembre 1583 nel Derbyshire, England come figlia di Rici Haynes e Ellan Haynes. Lei sposato Richardus Holmes il 30 gennaio 1603, nel Chesterfield, Derbyshire, Inghilterra, Regno Unito. Erano genitori di almeno 2 figlie. Morì nel Chesterfield, Derbyshire, Inghilterra, Regno Unito, e fu sepolta a Chesterfield, Derbyshire, England.

ja.lifeSummary: Margreta Haynesは1583年11月にイギリスデルブイシュアイアでRici HaynesとEllan Haynesの娘として生まれた。1603年1月30日にイギリスイギリスデルブイシュアイアチェステルフィールドでRichardus Holmesと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも2人の娘の親であった。彼女はイギリスイギリスデルブイシュアイアチェステルフィールドで死去し，イギリスデルブイシュアイアチェステルフィールドにて埋葬された。

ko.lifeSummary: Margreta Haynes(은)는 1583년 11월 영국 더비실르에서 Rici Haynes(와)과 Ellan Haynes의 딸로 태어났다. 그녀는 1603년 1월 30일 영국 잉글랜드 더비실르 체스터필드에서 Richardus Holmes(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 두 명의 딸을 두었다. 그녀는 영국 잉글랜드 더비실르 체스터필드에서 사망했으며 영국 더비실르 체스터필드에 묻혔다.

pt.lifeSummary: Margreta Haynes nasceu em novembro de 1583, em Derbyshire, Inglaterra filha de Rici Haynes e Ellan Haynes. Ela casou-se com Richardus Holmes em 30 de janeiro de 1603, em Chesterfield, Derbyshire, Inglaterra, United Kingdom. Eles tiveram pelo menos 2 filhas. Ela faleceu em Chesterfield, Derbyshire, Inglaterra, United Kingdom, e foi sepultada em Chesterfield, Derbyshire, Inglaterra.

ru.lifeSummary: Margreta Haynes родилась ноябрь 1583 г. в местности дирбишир, Англия. Её родителей звали Rici Haynes и Ellan Haynes. Она вышла замуж за человека по имени Richardus Holmes 30 января 1603 г. в местности честирфиелд, дирбишир, Англия, Великобритания. У них было по крайней мере 2 дочери. Она умерла в местности честирфиелд, дирбишир, Англия, Великобритания и была похоронена в местности честирфиелд, дирбишир, Англия.

es.lifeSummary: Margreta Haynes nació en noviembre de 1583, en Derbyshire, Inglaterra como hija de Rici Haynes y Ellan Haynes. Se casó con Richardus Holmes el 30 de enero de 1603, en Chesterfield, Derbyshire, Inglaterra, Reino Unido. Fueron padres de por lo menos 2 hijas. Murió en Chesterfield, Derbyshire, Inglaterra, Reino Unido, y fue sepultada en Chesterfield, Derbyshire, Inglaterra.
